conflict_type: Conflicts between Keyword Constraints and Phrase Constraints
seed_instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals.
org_constraint: Begin the first paragraph with the phrase "I hope this email finds you well."
new_constraint: The keyword "find" must be used exclusively as a noun.
instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals. Begin the first paragraph with the phrase "I hope this email finds you well." The keyword "find" must be used exclusively as a noun.
response:
Subject: Introduction - [Your Name] - [Course Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to introduce myself as a student in your [Course Name] class this semester. My name is [Your Name], and I am a [Your Year, e.g., sophomore, graduate student] majoring in [Your Major]. I have always been deeply interested in [mention a broad area related to the professor's field or the course], and I am particularly looking forward to diving into the material you cover in this course.

My academic goals are centered around [State your primary academic goal, e.g., developing a strong theoretical foundation in X, specializing in Y area of research]. Specifically, I am hoping that this course will help me [mention a specific skill or knowledge you hope to gain, e.g., refine my critical analysis skills, gain a deeper understanding of post-colonial theory]. Beyond the classroom, I am also interested in [mention a related extracurricular interest or career goal, e.g., pursuing research opportunities in sustainable energy, working in museum curation].

I am eager to contribute actively to class discussions and learn from your expertise. Please let me know if there is any preparatory reading or material you recommend I review before our first major assignment.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to a productive semester in your class.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number]<turn|>
